---
title: "Blackboard Setup Specification"
---

## Purpose

This appendix is the **deployment-ready specification** for building the eight BER 640 module quizzes in Blackboard. The quiz set **mirrors the eight homework assignments one-to-one** — one quiz per homework — so the eight quizzes vary in length and weight, right-sized to the homework each reinforces (**85 items / 114 points** in total). This appendix collects everything OTIDE needs in one place: the question-type mapping, the per-quiz deployment settings, the release schedule, the numeric-answer (tolerance) policy, and a complete **answer-key summary** for every item. Each quiz's content (stems, options, rationale) lives in its chapter; this appendix is the configuration layer on top of it.

::: {.callout-important}
## Items flagged for instructor confirmation
A few settings are **proposed defaults** (see the [Item-Writing Style Guide](style-guide.qmd)). Confirm before deployment: the **60-minute time limit**, the **answer-release timing** ("after the availability window closes"), and the **exact open/due calendar dates** (set from the official OTIDE Fall 2026 Course Schedule). Attempts (Unlimited), score handling (Highest), the 8-quiz set, and 10% weight are fixed by the course's mastery design.
:::

## Question-type mapping

Every item is one of six **auto-gradable** Blackboard question types, so quizzes score instantly and support unlimited attempts with no manual grading.

| Book item type | Blackboard question type | Notes |
|:---------------|:-------------------------|:------|
| Multiple Choice | **Multiple Choice** | One correct answer. Enable answer-order randomization. |
| Multiple Answer | **Multiple Answer** | "Select all that apply." Partial credit OFF (all-or-nothing) unless OTIDE prefers partial. |
| True/False | **True/False** | — |
| Matching | **Matching** | Enter each prompt → response pair. Enable order randomization. |
| Fill-in-the-Blank (numeric) | **Calculated Numeric** (or Fill in the Blank, Number) | Set the keyed value + the tolerance from the table below. |
| Fill-in-Multiple-Blanks | **Fill in Multiple Blanks** | One numeric blank per sub-answer, each with its own tolerance. |

## Master settings table

The same deployment settings apply to all eight quizzes except the availability week, item count, and points — each quiz is **right-sized to its paired homework**, so the per-quiz **Items** and **Points** differ (they are *not* a uniform total). The time limit (60 min), attempts (Unlimited), score kept (Highest), and answer-release timing are identical across all eight.

| Quiz | Module (↔ HW) | Notes Ch. | Available after | Open ≈ (Fall 2026) | Items | Points | Time limit | Attempts | Score kept | Show correct answers |
|:-----|:--------------|:----------|:----------------|:-------------------|:-----:|:------:|:----------:|:--------:|:-----------|:---------------------|
| Quiz 1 | Simple Linear Regression (HW1) | Ch 1 | Week 2 | early Sep | 9 | 12 | 60 min | Unlimited | Highest | After availability ends |
| Quiz 2 | Dummy Variables & the *t*-Test (HW2) | Ch 1 | Week 3 | early Sep | 9 | 12 | 60 min | Unlimited | Highest | After availability ends |
| Quiz 3 | Confounding & ANCOVA (HW3) | Ch 2 | Week 4 | mid Sep | 10 | 13 | 60 min | Unlimited | Highest | After availability ends |
| Quiz 4 | One-Way ANOVA & Multiple Comparisons (HW4) | Ch 3 | Week 5 | mid Sep | 12 | 15 | 60 min | Unlimited | Highest | After availability ends |
| Quiz 5 | Interactions & Simple Slopes (HW5) | Ch 4–5 | Week 7 | early Oct | 13 | 17 | 60 min | Unlimited | Highest | After availability ends |
| Quiz 6 | Two-Way ANOVA & Interaction (HW6) | Ch 5 | Week 8 | mid Oct | 11 | 16 | 60 min | Unlimited | Highest | After availability ends |
| Quiz 7 | Logistic Regression & Odds Ratios (HW7) | Ch 9–10 | Week 10 | late Oct | 10 | 14 | 60 min | Unlimited | Highest | After availability ends |
| Quiz 8 | Multiple Logistic & Simpson's Paradox (HW8) | Ch 10–11 | Week 11 | early Nov | 11 | 15 | 60 min | Unlimited | Highest | After availability ends |
| **Total** | **8 quizzes (one per homework)** | — | — | — | **85** | **114** | — | — | — | — |

: The "Open ≈" column is an approximate calendar guide only; the course runs Aug 19 – Dec 4, 2026, so the authoritative open/due dates come from the OTIDE Course Schedule (Camille sets the Grade Center dates). {.striped}

::: {.callout-note}
## Recommended Grade Center / feedback configuration
- **Multiple attempts:** Unlimited; **score kept = Highest**.
- **Per-attempt feedback:** show the **score** and **which questions were incorrect** after each submission — but **not** the correct answers, to preserve item integrity across unlimited retakes.
- **Show correct answers + rationale:** only **after the availability window closes**.
- **Question delivery:** randomize **question order** and **answer-option order** (neutralizes any answer-position pattern in the print source).
- **Weight:** the 8 quizzes together count **10%** of the final grade.
:::

## Per-quiz deployment tables

Build each quiz with the settings below. These reproduce the **Blackboard settings** table at the top of each quiz chapter; only **Number of questions**, **Total points**, and **Availability** change from quiz to quiz — everything else (60-minute limit, Unlimited attempts, Highest recorded, answers shown after the availability window) is identical across all eight.

::: {.panel-tabset}

### Quiz 1

| Setting | Value |
|:--------|:------|
| Number of questions | 9 |
| Total points | 12 |
| Time limit | 60 minutes |
| Attempts | Unlimited |
| Score recorded | Highest |
| Availability | After Week 2 |
| Show correct answers | After the availability window closes |

: Deployment settings for Quiz 1 — Simple Linear Regression (↔ HW1, Notes Ch. 1). {.striped}

### Quiz 2

| Setting | Value |
|:--------|:------|
| Number of questions | 9 |
| Total points | 12 |
| Time limit | 60 minutes |
| Attempts | Unlimited |
| Score recorded | Highest |
| Availability | After Week 3 |
| Show correct answers | After the availability window closes |

: Deployment settings for Quiz 2 — Dummy Variables & the *t*-Test (↔ HW2, Notes Ch. 1). {.striped}

### Quiz 3

| Setting | Value |
|:--------|:------|
| Number of questions | 10 |
| Total points | 13 |
| Time limit | 60 minutes |
| Attempts | Unlimited |
| Score recorded | Highest |
| Availability | After Week 4 |
| Show correct answers | After the availability window closes |

: Deployment settings for Quiz 3 — Confounding & ANCOVA (↔ HW3, Notes Ch. 2). {.striped}

### Quiz 4

| Setting | Value |
|:--------|:------|
| Number of questions | 12 |
| Total points | 15 |
| Time limit | 60 minutes |
| Attempts | Unlimited |
| Score recorded | Highest |
| Availability | After Week 5 |
| Show correct answers | After the availability window closes |

: Deployment settings for Quiz 4 — One-Way ANOVA & Multiple Comparisons (↔ HW4, Notes Ch. 3). {.striped}

### Quiz 5

| Setting | Value |
|:--------|:------|
| Number of questions | 13 |
| Total points | 17 |
| Time limit | 60 minutes |
| Attempts | Unlimited |
| Score recorded | Highest |
| Availability | After Week 7 |
| Show correct answers | After the availability window closes |

: Deployment settings for Quiz 5 — Interactions & Simple Slopes (↔ HW5, Notes Ch. 4–5). {.striped}

### Quiz 6

| Setting | Value |
|:--------|:------|
| Number of questions | 11 |
| Total points | 16 |
| Time limit | 60 minutes |
| Attempts | Unlimited |
| Score recorded | Highest |
| Availability | After Week 8 |
| Show correct answers | After the availability window closes |

: Deployment settings for Quiz 6 — Two-Way ANOVA & Interaction (↔ HW6, Notes Ch. 5). {.striped}

### Quiz 7

| Setting | Value |
|:--------|:------|
| Number of questions | 10 |
| Total points | 14 |
| Time limit | 60 minutes |
| Attempts | Unlimited |
| Score recorded | Highest |
| Availability | After Week 10 |
| Show correct answers | After the availability window closes |

: Deployment settings for Quiz 7 — Logistic Regression & Odds Ratios (↔ HW7, Notes Ch. 9–10). {.striped}

### Quiz 8

| Setting | Value |
|:--------|:------|
| Number of questions | 11 |
| Total points | 15 |
| Time limit | 60 minutes |
| Attempts | Unlimited |
| Score recorded | Highest |
| Availability | After Week 11 |
| Show correct answers | After the availability window closes |

: Deployment settings for Quiz 8 — Multiple Logistic & Simpson's Paradox (↔ HW8, Notes Ch. 10–11). {.striped}

:::

## Numeric-answer (tolerance) policy

For every Fill-in-the-Blank / Fill-in-Multiple-Blanks item, configure Blackboard's numeric answer with the **keyed value and tolerance** below. Where the stem asks for a *proportion*, accept the decimal form (not the percentage); where it asks for a percent, accept the number without the `%` sign.

| Quiz · Item | Keyed value | Accept range | Format note |
|:------------|:-----------:|:-------------|:------------|
| Q1 · 5 (FIMB) | 0 / 1 | exact (accept 1.0) | two numeric blanks; blank 3 is the phrase "exactly equals" |
| Q1 · 6 | 2.6 | 2.55 – 2.65 | tolerance $\pm 0.05$ |
| Q2 · 3 | 535.56 | 535.0 – 536.1 | tolerance $\approx \pm 0.5$ |
| Q2 · 6 (FIMB) | 4.65 / 1018 | 4.6 – 4.7 / exact | $t$-statistic (±0.05) and $df$ (exact); blank 3 is "reject" |
| Q3 · 2 | 2 | 1.9 – 2.1 | tolerance $\pm 0.1$ |
| Q3 · 5 (FIMB) | 11.8 / 11.2 / −0.6 | ±0.1 each | three numeric blanks; blank 3 is a negative difference |
| Q4 · 4 | 538.18 | 537.9 – 538.5 | tolerance $\pm 0.3$ |
| Q5 · 8 | −9504.21 | −9560 – −9450 | negative value; tolerance $\approx \pm 55$ |
| Q5 · 9 (FIMB) | 698.46 / 3932.71 | 690 – 707 / 3900 – 3965 | two numeric blanks; blank 3 is the word "widens" |
| Q5 · 13 | 5.0 | 4.9 – 5.1 | VIF; tolerance $\pm 0.1$ |
| Q6 · 2 (FIMB) | 14.72 / 2 / 4522 | 14.6 – 14.8 / exact / exact | $F$ (±0.1) and the two df (exact); blank 4 is "significant" |
| Q6 · 5 | 98.53 | 98.0 – 99.0 | tolerance $\pm 0.5$ |
| Q6 · 8 (FIMB) | 465.77 / 72.42 / 62.87 | 465.3 – 466.3 / 71.9 – 72.9 / 62.4 – 63.4 | three numeric blanks (cell-mean coefficients) |
| Q6 · 9 | 98.53 | 98.0 – 99.0 | tolerance $\pm 0.5$ |
| Q7 · 2 | 1.10 | 1.09 – 1.11 | odds ratio; tolerance $\pm 0.01$ |
| Q7 · 5 | 24 | 23 – 25 | percent change in odds; tolerance $\pm 1$ |
| Q7 · 7 (FIMB) | 0.275 / 0.576 | 0.27 – 0.28 / 0.57 – 0.58 | two numeric blanks (log-odds CI limits), ±0.01 each |
| Q8 · 1 | 1.71 | 1.68 – 1.74 | odds; tolerance $\pm 0.03$ |
| Q8 · 2 | 0.64 | 0.62 – 0.66 | crude odds ratio; tolerance $\pm 0.02$ |
| Q8 · 9 (FIMB) | 0.12 | 0.11 – 0.13 | one numeric blank (Wald $p$); blank 2 is "fail to reject" |

## Answer-key summary

Use these tables to verify the build and to spot-check Grade Center scoring. **Type** uses MC / MA / TF / MATCH / FIB / FIMB. (Question order and answer-option order are randomized at delivery, so the letters below refer to the source chapters, not necessarily what a student sees.)

### Quiz 1 — Simple Linear Regression (Week 2 · 9 items / 12 pts · ↔ HW1)

| # | Type | Key |
|:-:|:----:|:----|
| 1 | MC | B |
| 2 | MC | B |
| 3 | MATCH | 1→W, 2→V, 3→X, 4→Z, 5→Y |
| 4 | MC | C |
| 5 | FIMB | 0 / 1 / "exactly equals" |
| 6 | FIB | 2.6 |
| 7 | MC | A |
| 8 | TF | False |
| 9 | MC | A |

### Quiz 2 — Dummy Variables & the *t*-Test (Week 3 · 9 items / 12 pts · ↔ HW2)

| # | Type | Key |
|:-:|:----:|:----|
| 1 | MATCH | 1→Z, 2→X, 3→Y |
| 2 | MC | C |
| 3 | FIB | 535.56 |
| 4 | MC | B |
| 5 | TF | True |
| 6 | FIMB | 4.65 / 1018 / "reject" |
| 7 | TF | True |
| 8 | MC | D |
| 9 | TF | False |

### Quiz 3 — Confounding & ANCOVA (Week 4 · 10 items / 13 pts · ↔ HW3)

| # | Type | Key |
|:-:|:----:|:----|
| 1 | MC | A |
| 2 | FIB | 2 |
| 3 | MC | A |
| 4 | MC | C |
| 5 | FIMB | 11.8 / 11.2 / −0.6 |
| 6 | MC | C |
| 7 | TF | False |
| 8 | MC | B |
| 9 | TF | False |
| 10 | MA | A, B, C |

### Quiz 4 — One-Way ANOVA & Multiple Comparisons (Week 5 · 12 items / 15 pts · ↔ HW4)

| # | Type | Key |
|:-:|:----:|:----|
| 1 | MC | B |
| 2 | TF | False |
| 3 | MC | C |
| 4 | FIB | 538.18 |
| 5 | MC | A |
| 6 | TF | False |
| 7 | MATCH | 1→Y, 2→W, 3→X, 4→Z |
| 8 | TF | True |
| 9 | MC | B |
| 10 | MA | A, B, C |
| 11 | MC | C |
| 12 | MC | C |

### Quiz 5 — Interactions & Simple Slopes (Week 7 · 13 items / 17 pts · ↔ HW5)

| # | Type | Key |
|:-:|:----:|:----|
| 1 | MC | A |
| 2 | MC | C |
| 3 | MC | B |
| 4 | TF | False |
| 5 | MC | C |
| 6 | TF | False |
| 7 | MC | D |
| 8 | FIB | −9504.21 |
| 9 | FIMB | 698.46 / 3932.71 / "widens" |
| 10 | TF | False |
| 11 | MC | A |
| 12 | MATCH | 1→X, 2→W, 3→Y |
| 13 | FIB | 5.0 |

### Quiz 6 — Two-Way ANOVA & Interaction (Week 8 · 11 items / 16 pts · ↔ HW6)

| # | Type | Key |
|:-:|:----:|:----|
| 1 | MC | A |
| 2 | FIMB | 14.72 / 2 / 4522 / "significant" |
| 3 | TF | False |
| 4 | MC | C |
| 5 | FIB | 98.53 |
| 6 | MC | B |
| 7 | TF | True |
| 8 | FIMB | 465.77 / 72.42 / 62.87 |
| 9 | FIB | 98.53 |
| 10 | MC | C |
| 11 | MATCH | 1→W, 2→X, 3→Y, 4→Z |

### Quiz 7 — Logistic Regression & Odds Ratios (Week 10 · 10 items / 14 pts · ↔ HW7)

| # | Type | Key |
|:-:|:----:|:----|
| 1 | MATCH | 1→Z, 2→X, 3→Y |
| 2 | FIB | 1.10 |
| 3 | MC | B |
| 4 | MC | C |
| 5 | FIB | 24 |
| 6 | MC | D |
| 7 | FIMB | 0.275 / 0.576 |
| 8 | TF | False |
| 9 | TF | False |
| 10 | TF | True |

### Quiz 8 — Multiple Logistic & Simpson's Paradox (Week 11 · 11 items / 15 pts · ↔ HW8)

| # | Type | Key |
|:-:|:----:|:----|
| 1 | FIB | 1.71 |
| 2 | FIB | 0.64 |
| 3 | MC | B |
| 4 | MC | C |
| 5 | TF | True |
| 6 | MC | C |
| 7 | MC | D |
| 8 | TF | False |
| 9 | FIMB | 0.12 / "fail to reject" |
| 10 | MC | A |
| 11 | MATCH | 1→Y, 2→W, 3→Z, 4→X |

## Mastery grading scale

Each quiz is scored on **its own point total** (12–17 points, see the master settings table) and converted to a percentage; the Grade Center keeps the **highest** percentage across a student's unlimited attempts. Because the quizzes are right-sized to their paired homework, the mastery bands are stated as **percentages of each quiz's own denominator**, not as a fixed point cutoff. The bands (per the course [syllabus](../../assignment/quizzes.html)) are:

| Score | Criteria |
|:------|:---------|
| Full credit | 80% or higher of the quiz total |
| Partial credit | 60–79% of the quiz total |
| Minimal credit | Below 60% of the quiz total |
| No credit | Quiz not attempted |

The eight quizzes together contribute **10%** of the final course grade.
